target.title: docs: update changelog for v2026.6.10
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The existing `CHANGELOG.md` section for `v2026.6.10` needs to be replaced with the approved reader-facing release story. Without this update, the public changelog does not reflect the reviewed release narrative or its complete inline coverage of the shipped user-facing changes.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR replaces only the `v2026.6.10` changelog section with the approved public story from the release-notes workflow. It keeps all PR, issue, and verified contributor coverage represented inline and preserves the public changelog boundary by excluding audit-only release material.

The semantic comparison warnings are review context, not changes to this scope. The release-verification block is intentionally separate remote release metadata, and the automatic fast-mode section is the chosen detailed public presentation for this approved iteration.

## User Impact

OpenClaw users and readers can find the approved `v2026.6.10` release story in the public changelog, including the user-visible outcomes, source references, and contributor attribution for the covered work. The changelog continues to present public release information rather than maintainer-only accounting or remote release verification details.

## Evidence

- `publication-comparison.json` reports `approved-with-warnings`, confirms coverage of all 12 PRs, six related issues, and every verified contributor inline, and finds no material contradiction.
- `machine-evaluation.json` reports `passed`, including complete accounting for 291 release changes, 12 PRs, and 279 direct commits, with all listed structural validators passing.
- `llm-evaluation.json` reports `approved`; all quality gates are true and the findings list is empty.
- `human-review.json` records `reviewDecision: approved` and states that the iteration is approved for draft PR publication.
target.author: hannesrudolph
